summary: 2026-06-25 14:17:29 PDT - Re-reviewed only `git diff 726d919...HEAD`, `git log --oneline 726d919..HEAD`, and `reviews/api-log-redaction.md`. The scalar-only client-error fix correctly drops nested objects/arrays/functions under allowlisted keys and keeps finite numeric line/column values, with focused tests. The privacy-note fix is improved but still makes an absolute claim about participant data in error reports that is not supported by the intentionally retained string error fields.

findings.title: Privacy note still overstates error-report guarantee
findings.claim: The revised note says error reports “do not include participant names or other meeting data,” but the fix intentionally still forwards string `message`, `stack`, and `componentStack` values in `client/src/lib/reportError.js` with only length caps. That means a thrown error's own text can still contain a participant name or meeting value, which the spec accepts as residual risk; the public note should not promise the data is impossible to include.
findings.suggestion: Keep the diagnostics/no-values claim, but qualify the error-report sentence to say error reports are limited to error text plus fixed technical fields and do not attach arbitrary participant payloads or meeting-data fields.
